title: Course Schedule
description:
There are `numCourses` courses labeled `0` to `numCourses - 1`. Some have
prerequisites given as pairs `[a, b]` meaning you must take `b` before `a`.
Return `true` if you can finish all courses (the prerequisite graph has no
cycle), otherwise `false`.

solutions.summary: Repeatedly remove courses with no remaining prerequisites; a leftover means a cycle.

steps.title: Build graph + indegrees
steps.explanation: Record who depends on each course and how many prerequisites each course still needs.

code.python:
def can_finish(num_courses, prerequisites):
    graph = [[] for _ in range(num_courses)]
    indegree = [0] * num_courses
    for a, b in prerequisites:
        graph[b].append(a)
        indegree[a] += 1
    return True

steps.title: Peel off zero-indegree courses
steps.explanation: Start from courses with no prerequisites; each time one is taken, decrement its dependents. If everyone gets taken, there is no cycle.

code.python:
def can_finish(num_courses, prerequisites):
    graph = [[] for _ in range(num_courses)]
    indegree = [0] * num_courses
    for a, b in prerequisites:
        graph[b].append(a)
        indegree[a] += 1
    from collections import deque
    queue = deque(i for i in range(num_courses) if indegree[i] == 0)
    seen = 0
    while queue:
        node = queue.popleft()
        seen += 1
        for nxt in graph[node]:
            indegree[nxt] -= 1
            if indegree[nxt] == 0:
                queue.append(nxt)
    return seen == num_courses
